itineraries.name: The Ten Crus loop: Brouilly to Saint-Amour in three days
itineraries.theme: All ten Beaujolais Crus from the volcanic south to the granite north

days.morning: Begin at Mont Brouilly, climbing to the chapel at the summit for a panorama over Brouilly and Cote de Brouilly, then taste at Chateau Thivin in Odenas.
days.lunch: Lunch in Odenas or drive north to Belleville-en-Beaujolais for a bouchon lunch.
days.afternoon: Drive north to Régnié-Durette for the tenth and newest Cru, then continue to Villié-Morgon for the afternoon and a walk up the Cote du Py granite hill.
days.evening: Evening in Belleville-en-Beaujolais with a glass of Morgon at a local wine bar.

days.morning: Morning in Villié-Morgon: visit at a Morgon domaine by appointment, then drive north through Chiroubles to Fleurie for a mid-morning tasting.
days.afternoon: Drive north through Moulin-a-Vent and Chenas to Saint-Amour, the northernmost Cru, tasting at small producers along the route.

days.morning: Return south through the Beaujolais-Villages zone for a comparative tasting at a cooperative or negociant showing the full appellation range.
days.lunch: Picnic in the vineyards near Beaujeu, the historic capital.
days.afternoon: Visit Beaujeu town: the medieval streets, the Maison du Pays du Beaujolais, and a final Cru tasting before the drive south.
days.evening: Drive to Lyon for a bouchon dinner serving the Beaujolais you have just tasted in its natural restaurant environment.

itineraries.description: A three-day circuit from Brouilly in the south to Saint-Amour in the north taking in all ten Beaujolais Cru appellations on a route that passes through the granite heartland of Morgon and Fleurie and ends with a Lyon bouchon dinner.
itineraries.tip: Book domaine visits in Morgon and Fleurie at least two weeks ahead; most small producers do not have permanent tasting rooms and receive visitors by appointment only.

itineraries.name: Gang of Four Morgon natural-wine deep dive, two days
itineraries.theme: The origins of the natural-wine movement: Morgon and Villié-Morgon

days.morning: Arrive in Villié-Morgon and walk the Cote du Py volcanic schist hill before a morning appointment at Domaine Lapierre.
days.afternoon: Afternoon visit at Jean Foillard, whose Morgon Cote du Py represents the most mineral expression of the hill, followed by an evening tasting at Guy Breton.
days.evening: Dinner in the village using ingredients from the local market and a bottle of Morgon opened in the cellar.

days.morning: Morning appointment at Jean-Paul Thévenet, the fourth member of the original Gang, whose Morgon Vielles Vignes comes from very old Gobelet vines.
days.lunch: Lunch in Villié-Morgon before a final walk through the village to understand how concentrated the natural-wine movement is within this single commune.
days.afternoon: Drive east to Lyon for an afternoon exploration of the natural-wine bars where Morgon became an international reference, ending with a bouchon dinner.
days.evening: Natural-wine bar in Lyon's Presqu'ile neighbourhood.

itineraries.description: A two-day Morgon deep dive into the village where Marcel Lapierre, Jean Foillard, Guy Breton, and Jean-Paul Thévenet created the natural-wine movement, anchored by appointments at each of the four original domaines and ending with Lyon's bouchon culture.
itineraries.tip: All four domaines require appointments and most do not have formal visitor infrastructure; contact each producer directly months ahead and confirm by email that your visit is confirmed.

itineraries.name: Lyon, Beaujolais, and Maconnais: a three-day wine weekend
itineraries.theme: From Burgundy's Chardonnay south through Beaujolais Gamay to Lyon's bouchons

days.morning: Arrive in Lyon and spend the morning exploring the Presqu'ile natural-wine bar scene, tasting Beaujolais Cru by the glass before any winery visit.
days.lunch: Lunch at a Lyon bouchon serving Beaujolais from the pot lyonnais.
days.afternoon: Drive north to the Beaujolais: first stop Fleurie village, tasting at a small Fleurie producer, then drive to Villié-Morgon for a late afternoon walk.

days.morning: Morning appointment at a Morgon domaine followed by a drive north to Moulin-a-Vent for comparison between the two most structured Crus.
days.afternoon: Continue north into the Maconnais, tasting Pouilly-Fuissé at the Roche de Solutre and comparing the limestone Chardonnay with the granite Gamay just tasted.
days.evening: Dinner in Macon or drive back to Belleville.

days.morning: Drive back to Lyon via the Pierres Dorees southern Beaujolais, stopping at a golden-stone village such as Oingt before a final morning wine-bar visit in Lyon.
days.lunch: Final bouchon lunch in Lyon's Vieux-Lyon.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at leisure in Lyon before departure.

itineraries.description: A three-day wine-country weekend that uses Lyon as a base and hub, moving north through the Beaujolais Crus into the Maconnais Chardonnay country and returning via the medieval Pierres Dorees villages.
itineraries.tip: Lyon has a direct TGV connection to Paris (2h) and to most European capitals; the Beaujolais is 40 min by car from the city centre, making a Lyon-based weekend the most flexible logistics for a Beaujolais trip.

itineraries.name: Harvest week cellar pilgrimage, early September
itineraries.theme: Whole-cluster carbonic maceration, harvest lunches, and cellar immersion across the Crus

days.morning: Arrive in Villié-Morgon during the third or fourth week of September when Morgon harvest is typically underway. Walk the vineyards before the morning picking crew starts.
days.lunch: Harvest crew lunch at a welcoming domaine (arranged by prior invitation).
days.afternoon: Afternoon in the cellar watching whole-cluster sorting and the start of carbonic maceration vats.
days.evening: Evening glass of the previous vintage with the growers.

days.morning: Morning at a Fleurie domaine during their later harvest (usually one week behind Morgon). See the earlier-ripening Fleurie sand-granite terroir at work.
days.afternoon: Drive to Moulin-a-Vent, the last Cru to harvest, and taste the previous year's structured Moulin-a-Vent from tank.
days.evening: Dinner at a village restaurant open during harvest.

days.morning: Visit Chateau Thivin on Mont Brouilly for a harvest-morning walk through the volcanic diorite vineyards at the top of the appellation.
days.lunch: Picnic on the Mont Brouilly slopes with a previous-vintage Cote de Brouilly.
days.afternoon: Drive back to Morgon for an afternoon barrel-tasting session of the just-fermenting juice in one of the Gang of Four cellars.

days.morning: Morning at a cooperative or négociant to understand the scale of the Nouveau production operation alongside the Cru program.
days.afternoon: Drive south to Lyon for departure.
days.evening: Lyon bouchon dinner with a Morgon Cru from the cellar.

itineraries.description: A four-day September harvest immersion across four Cru appellations, combining cellar and vineyard access during the picking weeks with comparative Cru tastings from previous vintages.
itineraries.tip: Harvest dates vary significantly by vintage and Cru; contact producers in August to confirm their expected picking dates before booking accommodation.

itineraries.name: Pierres Dorees golden-stone village and southern Beaujolais weekend
itineraries.theme: Medieval golden-stone villages, limestone-soil Gamay, and the southern Beaujolais off the tourist circuit

days.morning: Arrive in Oingt, one of the most beautiful medieval villages in France, and walk the village fortifications before a morning tasting at Domaine des Terres Dorees in Charnay.
days.lunch: Lunch at a village restaurant in Oingt or Theizé.
days.afternoon: Drive the Route des Pierres Dorees via Chatillon d'Azergues, Salles-Arbuissonnas, and the honey-stone villages that give the sub-region its name.
days.evening: Evening aperitif with the local Beaujolais blanc (Chardonnay) at a domaine courtyard tasting.
days.accommodation: Rural gite or chambre d'hotes in the Pierres Dorees.

days.morning: Morning drive north to Villefranche-sur-Saone, the commercial capital of the Beaujolais, for the Saturday morning market and the Maison du Beaujolais wine shop.
days.afternoon: Drive back south to Lyon via the Saone valley.
days.evening: Lyon dinner with a Beaujolais from the Pierres Dorees producer just visited.

itineraries.description: A two-day exploration of the southern Beaujolais limestone country and its golden-stone medieval villages, anchored by Jean-Paul Brun's Domaine des Terres Dorees and the Pierres Dorees Route Touristique.
itineraries.tip: The Pierres Dorees is almost never crowded even in summer; it is the most relaxed part of the Beaujolais and genuinely unknown to most wine tourists who focus exclusively on the Cru belt.
